title: primula villosa subsp. commutata
intro: Primula villosa subsp. commutata, also known as the hairy primrose or primrose of the hills, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the primrose family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. The health benefits of Primula villosa subsp. commutata include its potential to treat coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues such as indigestion and diarrhea. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a versatile addition to natural medicine practices.
